Class User

java.lang.Object
models.User
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<User>
Direct Known Subclasses:
Employee, ResearcherDecorator, Student
public abstract class User extends Object implements Serializable, Comparable<User>
Abstract base class representing a user in the university system.

All user roles (Student, Teacher, Admin, Manager, etc.) extend this class. It provides shared identity fields, account status management, and a natural ordering by username (case-insensitive).

Implements Serializable so that user objects can be persisted to disk via the UniversityRepository.

See Also:
  • Student
  • Teacher
  • Admin

  • Constructor Details

    • User

      protected User(String id, String firstName, String lastName, String username, String passwordHash)
      Constructs a new User with the given identity credentials. The account is active by default.
      Parameters:
      id - unique identifier for this user (must not be null)
      firstName - user's first name
      lastName - user's last name
      username - login username (must be unique across the system)
      passwordHash - SHA-256 hex digest of the user's password

  • Method Details

    • getRole

      public abstract String getRole()
      Returns the role label for this user (e.g. "STUDENT", "TEACHER"). Subclasses must provide a concrete implementation.
      Returns:
      non-null role string identifying the user's type

    • getFullName

      public String getFullName()
      Returns the user's full name as "firstName lastName".
      Returns:
      full name string

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this user.
      Returns:
      user ID (never null)

    • getFirstName

      public String getFirstName()
      Returns the user's first name.
      Returns:
      first name

    • setFirstName

      public void setFirstName(String firstName)
      Updates the user's first name.
      Parameters:
      firstName - new first name (must not be null)

    • getLastName

      public String getLastName()
      Returns the user's last name.
      Returns:
      last name

    • setLastName

      public void setLastName(String lastName)
      Updates the user's last name.
      Parameters:
      lastName - new last name (must not be null)

    • getUsername

      public String getUsername()
      Returns the user's login username.
      Returns:
      username

    • setUsername

      public void setUsername(String username)
      Updates the login username. Callers are responsible for ensuring uniqueness.
      Parameters:
      username - new username

    • getPasswordHash

      public String getPasswordHash()
      Returns the stored password hash (SHA-256 hex).
      Returns:
      password hash string

    • setPasswordHash

      public void setPasswordHash(String passwordHash)
      Replaces the stored password hash.
      Parameters:
      passwordHash - new SHA-256 hex digest

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()
      Returns true if this account is active and may log in.
      Returns:
      true if active, false if deactivated

    • setActive

      public void setActive(boolean active)
      Enables or disables this account.
      Parameters:
      active - true to activate, false to deactivate

    • getLastLoginAt

      public LocalDateTime getLastLoginAt()
      Returns the timestamp of the last successful login.
      Returns:
      last login time, or null if the user has never logged in

    • setLastLoginAt

      public void setLastLoginAt(LocalDateTime lastLoginAt)
      Records the time of the most recent login. Typically set by AuthService.
      Parameters:
      lastLoginAt - login timestamp

    • getDeactivationReason

      public String getDeactivationReason()
      Returns the reason this account was deactivated, if any.
      Returns:
      deactivation reason, or null if none was provided

    • setDeactivationReason

      public void setDeactivationReason(String deactivationReason)
      Sets a human-readable explanation for why the account was deactivated. This message is shown to the user on a failed login attempt.
      Parameters:
      deactivationReason - explanation text (may be null)

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(User other)
      Compares this user to another by username (case-insensitive alphabetical order).
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<User>
      Parameters:
      other - the other user
      Returns:
      negative, zero, or positive as this username comes before, equals, or comes after the other username

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Two users are equal if and only if their IDs are equal.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - object to compare
      Returns:
      true if o is a User with the same ID

      public int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a concise string representation of this user for debugging.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      string with id, full name, username, role, active flag, and last login
